q : Page fault occurred
r : Error occurred

S2 : “Error occurred if and only if program requested a page and page fault occurred”.
S3 : “Error occurred”.

NAT

Solution

1

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the directed graph given below:

 
Which of the following is TRUE?
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
In finding topological ordering of any graph
1. Choose vertex which has ‘0’ indegree. If not present ‘0’ indegree vertex then topological ordering not possible.
2. If ‘0’ degree node present then, remove it and find next “zero” indegree vertex.
3. If ‘0’ degree node present then, remove it and find next “zero” indegree vertex.
4. Topological ordering is : “DMACBEYS”.

Q20 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Let (G, *) be a group such that O(G) = 8, where O(G) denotes the order of the group G. Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a)
Theorem: If a is an element of G, then O(a) must divide O(G).
Therefore (a) is true, as O(a) = 6 and 6 doesn’t divide 8.
However the converse of the above theorem need not be true, therefore (b) is false.
(c) says there are at least two elements in G with order 1, however this violates the fact that every
group can have only one identity element. So (c) is also wrong. So the appropriate choice is (d).

Q22 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are false?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
(Z, +) is both group and Abelian group, as it satisfies commutative property and inverse element
is –a ∀a ∈ Z.
(Z, –) is never semigroup, because subtraction operation is not associative and hence cannot be
monoid too.
(Z, ×) is monoid but not group, because inverse does not exist i.e. for any integer ‘a’ it’s inverse is
1/a which is a rational number.
Hence it is monoid only.
